overall_summary: Alexandre Figueras, operating Monza Tech's Sentinel Grid platform, argues that forward-looking grid stress prediction using publicly available data is technically viable today and could inform interconnection study processes. The comment offers to provide technical input to FERC as it considers large load interconnection rules.

quotes.text: forward-looking grid stress prediction, using publicly available data, is technically viable today and could inform interconnection study processes.
quotes.concern: Grid stress forecasting tools exist now and could be used in interconnection studies

quotes.text: We score daily failure probability across 6 US RTOs using EIA demand, weather, and LMP congestion data. Our walk-forward validated model detects 87% of reliability events with a 13% false positive rate.
quotes.concern: Claims high accuracy for a multi-RTO reliability prediction model built on public data

bins.name: Forecasting tools for studies
bins.description: The filer contends that probabilistic grid reliability forecasting, built on public EIA, weather, and LMP data, is accurate enough today to be incorporated into interconnection study processes.

bins.name: RTO reliability prediction platform
bins.description: Monza Tech's Sentinel Grid scores daily failure probability across six RTOs, detecting 87% of reliability events with a 13% false positive rate, and offers this capability as potential technical input to FERC rulemaking.
